top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — 35M+ FOOD INSECURE (continuing Jul 6): WFP warns of imminent food assistance cuts as violence and hunger surge. 35M projected acutely food insecure nationwide — worst in a decade. 15,000 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno; 750,000 in severe hunger. 3M children under 5 face life-threatening severe acute malnutrition. WFP can reach fewer than half of previous year's beneficiaries across NE states. Communities joining armed groups for food. Obi (Labour Party leader) and VP expressed concern over UN warnings Jul 4. FAO/WFP joint briefing Jul 9 scheduled.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW — GLOBAL CRACKDOWN FEARS (Jul 6): ANI News Jul 6: "China's ethnic unity law raises fear of expanded global crackdown on dissent." Law in force since Jul 1; senior officials at Jun 24 State Council press confirmed extraterritorial applicability. Overseas individuals/organizations can be held liable for "undermining ethnic unity." Amnesty, UN OHCHR, European Parliament condemned law as "legal blueprint for cultural erasure" targeting Uyghurs, Tibetans, Mongolians. CNN Jul 1: "China tells minorities to assimilate." Taiwan government issued formal response. Germany Foreign Ministry expressed "great concern."

top_entities.name: Lebanon
top_entities.news_summary: IDF STRIKES — CONTINUING VIOLATIONS (Jul 6 window): IDF confirmed strike on car in Nabatieh al-Fawqa killing 4 suspects; Times of Israel liveblog Jul 3 documents ongoing strikes in Bint Jbeil, Beit Yahoun, Kounine, Baraashit. IDF says it struck Hezbollah weapons sites and Radwan Force training site. Lebanese President Aoun condemned "continuing violations" of April truce. 4,000+ killed since March 2 resumption; 1.2M+ displaced (>20% population). 76% of south Lebanon farmers displaced; 1M+ facing food insecurity. Agricultural damage at 22%.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: EARTHQUAKE DAY 12 (Jul 6) — TOLL 3,300+: Official and media figures now converge on 3,300+ confirmed dead, 16,400+ injured, 50,000+ unaccounted for. Forensic pathologists warn true toll may be 3× the official figure. UN estimated $4.7–8.7B in direct damage (~4–8% of GDP). 189 buildings completely levelled of 800 collapsed. WFP appealed $50M; UNICEF supplies for 100,000 in-country. Monsoon season risk intensifying. Venezuelan government $200M reconstruction fund covers <1% of estimated need. Ongoing search-and-rescue operations hampered by rubble and displaced infrastructure.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: KYIV STRIKES (Jul 5–6) — RECORD CIVILIAN TOLL: Ballistic missiles + drones hit Kyiv Jul 5–6, killing at least 15 in the city and 6 in surrounding region, 30+ wounded; Podilsky district hardest hit. None of 23 ballistic missiles intercepted (interceptor shortage). Ukraine average 170 civilians killed or injured daily in July 2026 — "significantly higher" than 2025, UN Human Rights Monitoring Mission. Russia also struck Dnipro (17 killed) and Zaporizhzhia/Kherson (4 killed, 37 wounded). EU Special Tribunal for crime of aggression operational. Japan announced $6B support package for Ukraine.

top_entities.name: Mali
top_entities.news_summary: JNIM SIEGE ONGOING + NEW COORDINATED ATTACKS JUL 4: ACLED/Bellingcat Jul analysis: JNIM's fuel blockade is strangling Bamako — security sources say organization is tightening siege, choking critical supplies. Jul 4: coordinated attacks in Anefis, Kenioroba, southwest Mali — marking new stage in southward expansion. JNIM fighters attacked Bourem, Bamako, Kati, Sévaré, Senou, Mopti since April 28 onset. HRW Jun 28 (in window): "grave abuses amid renewed fighting" — civilian executions documented. 5M in humanitarian need; response only 18.5% funded. FAO/WFP Sahel briefing Jul 9 forthcoming.

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: IPC PHASE 5 FAMINE RISK (ongoing Jul 6): 7.8M at IPC Phase 3+ (56% of population). 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate — in Akobo, Fangak, Nyirol, Uror (Jonglei) and Luakpiny/Nasir, Ulang (Upper Nile). Credible famine risk in four counties. 300,000 displaced in Jonglei; 200,000 from Akobo since March 6. 2.2M children acutely malnourished; 700,000 projected severe acute malnutrition through July. DRC Ebola now in Haut-Uele province (borders South Sudan). Cholera, malaria, measles compounding.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE CONTINUING (Jul 6): DOJ criminal investigation has expanded beyond Medicare billing to include Optum Rx prescription practices and physician reimbursement arrangements (PYMNTS). Civil probe: inflated diagnoses for higher Medicare Advantage payments. Senate Grassley investigation finding UHG "aggressively gaming" Medicare Advantage. Q2 earnings call July 29 — DOJ investigation status expected to be addressed. Court approved DOJ settlement on UHG-Amedisys merger (Apr 2026). Multiple Medicare Advantage kickback lawsuits active. Third-party review in progress (complete by end Q3).

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: RSF EL OBEID SIEGE — AL JAZEERA JUL 6: Al Jazeera published Jul 6: "El-Obeid under siege by RSF: Could this be Sudan's next el-Fasher?" — RSF systematically tightening siege. Amnesty International launched "City Under Siege, Children Under Fire" report Jul 1 documenting RSF crimes against humanity in North Darfur. UN Human Rights Council held landmark session Jul 3 to prevent El Obeid atrocities (HRW). HRW Jul 3: RSF drone strikes hit 16+ civilian targets including hospitals, schools, power stations; 45 civilians killed in 15 drone attacks Jun 6–28. Washington Post and UN News covered UN "red alert" (Jul 3). 500,000 civilians + 105,000 IDPs at siege risk.

top_entities.name: Palestine
top_entities.news_summary: CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS CONTINUE (Jul 6): Al Jazeera reports at least 6 Palestinians killed in Gaza on Jul 6 alone — drone attack on vehicle in Khan Younis (2 dead), tent in al-Mawasi (2 dead), apartment in Gaza City (married couple). Middle East Monitor: 5 more killed, 25 injured in separate Jul 6 strikes. Total killed since Oct 2025 ceasefire: 1,072; wounded: 3,463. Israel has also documented 3,500+ ceasefire violations. Israel controls >70% of Gaza. Hamas announced resignation of Gaza government Jul 6, transferring to US-backed technocratic committee. ICJ proceedings ongoing. UN Security Council July agenda active.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: KHAMENEI FUNERAL ONGOING — BURIAL JUL 9; US TALKS RESUME JUL 11: Mojtaba Khamenei (new Supreme Leader, confirmed Mar 9) remained absent from father's six-day state funeral amid reported severe injuries (leg amputation rumoured, facial disfigurement). Funeral continues in Tehran; burial scheduled Mashhad Jul 9. Trump paused US-Iran nuclear + Hormuz talks "for funeral period" — scheduled to resume Jul 11. NCRI Jun 27–Jul 4 execution surge report: 134 prisoners executed in a single month, 20 in 48 hours. Iran HRM Monthly Report Jun 2026: 141 executions in June alone. Political prisoner Arghavan Fallahi sentenced to death Jul 1. Execution YTD: 784+ (37-year high).

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: KYIV ATTACKS (Jul 5–6): Russia launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting Kyiv, killing 15+ in the city and 6 in surrounding region. Russia also struck Dnipro (17 killed), Zaporizhzhia and Kherson regions (4 killed, 37 wounded). July 2026 civilian toll: 170/day average — highest rate since 2023, significantly above 2025. EU Council sanctioned 9 Bucha massacre individuals. EU Special Tribunal for crime of aggression operational. 20,500+ Ukrainian children forcibly deported. EU Russia sanctions extended to July 2027.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS — MULTIPLE KILLS JUL 6: Al Jazeera Jul 6 reports IDF killing at least 6 Palestinians in Gaza including drone attack on tent sheltering displaced people in al-Mawasi. IDF also struck car in Nabatieh al-Fawqa, Lebanon, killing 4. Israel controls >70% of Gaza; Netanyahu stated IDF will not evacuate to pre-war lines. Israel previously announced enforcement of ban on 37 humanitarian organisations (effective March 2026). IDF Chief: "Yellow Line will be Israel's new border in Gaza Strip." Hamas government resigned Jul 6, transferring to US-backed committee. ICJ proceedings ongoing.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: EBOLA BUNDIBUGYO — 1,561 CONFIRMED, 506 DEAD (Jul 6 WHO update): As of Jul 6, 1,561 confirmed cases with 506 deaths — up from 1,406/438 on Jul 3 (averaging 52+ new confirmed per day). Ituri province most affected: 1,417 cases, 424 deaths across 24 of 36 health zones. 628 hospitalised; 254 recovered. International spread: France (1 imported case, Jun 24); Uganda (20 confirmed, contained). UN News Jul 6: healing from stigma on Uganda frontline. No approved vaccine or treatment for Bundibugyo strain. PHEIC active. M23/FARDC ongoing conflict; 7.2M internally displaced.

top_entities.name: Haiti
top_entities.news_summary: TPS WORK AUTHORIZATIONS EXPIRE JUL 10 — 350K HAITIANS AT RISK: DHS announced TPS work authorizations (Employment Authorization Documents) expire Jul 10, 2026. Supreme Court 6-3 ruling (Jun 25, Mullin v. Doe) cleared Trump administration to terminate TPS for ~350,000 Haitians — they revert to illegal status, face deportation. NPR Jul 2: US healthcare system impact highlighted as Haitian healthcare workers lose authorization. Florida Republican Carlos Gimenez called deportations a "huge mistake." Deportees face kidnapping/extortion risks as gangs control ~90% of Port-au-Prince. 1.5M+ internally displaced; 5.8M (52%) at crisis food insecurity.

sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026 (Day 12): Confirmed dead now 3,300+, injured 16,400+, missing 50,000+. Forensic pathologists warn true toll may be 3× official. UN estimated $4.7–8.7B direct damage (4–8% of GDP). 189 buildings completely levelled. WFP $50M appeal; UNICEF supplies for 100,000 in-country. Monsoon season risk intensifying; monsoon rains could collapse unstable structures and exacerbate displacement. Venezuelan government $200M reconstruction fund covers <1% of need.

sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026: Al Jazeera published analysis asking if El Obeid will become 'Sudan's next el-Fasher.' Amnesty International launched 'City Under Siege, Children Under Fire' report on July 1 documenting RSF crimes against humanity in North Darfur. UN Human Rights Council held emergency session July 3. HRW documented 45 civilians killed in 15 drone attacks June 6–28; food prices up 300%. 500,000 civilians + 105,000 IDPs at risk.

sector_alerts.title: Iran: Khamenei burial Jul 9 in Mashhad; Mojtaba still absent; US-Iran talks resume Jul 11; 141 executions in June
sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026: Six-day Khamenei state funeral continues in Tehran; burial scheduled Mashhad July 9. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei still absent — reportedly recovering from severe injuries (leg amputation rumoured). Trump granted 'week off' for funeral period; US-Iran nuclear/Hormuz talks scheduled to resume July 11. Iran HRM: 141 executions in June 2026 alone (37-year high). Political prisoner Arghavan Fallahi sentenced to death July 1. Execution YTD: 784+. GCC entities remain exposed during succession transition.

sector_alerts.title: DRC Ebola: 1,561 confirmed, 506 dead as of Jul 6 — fastest 3-day growth yet; no vaccine/treatment
sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026: WHO/CDC report 1,561 confirmed cases with 506 deaths as of July 6 — up from 1,406/438 on July 3 (averaging 52 new confirmed per day, accelerating). Ituri province: 1,417 cases. France imported case June 24 (in window). No approved vaccine or treatment for Bundibugyo strain. PHEIC active. UN News Jul 6: Uganda tackling frontline stigma in recovery phase. South Sudan cross-border risk via Haut-Uele continues.

sector_alerts.title: Haiti TPS: DHS work authorizations expire Jul 10 — 350,000 Haitians face immediate illegal status, deportation
sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026: DHS announced Employment Authorization Documents for Haitian TPS holders expire July 10, 2026 — four days away. Supreme Court 6-3 ruling (Mullin v. Doe, Jun 25) allows termination of TPS for ~350,000 Haitians; they revert to illegal status and face deportation. NPR: healthcare system impact as Haitian healthcare workers lose authorization. Rep. Carlos Gimenez (R-FL) called deportations a 'huge mistake.' Deportees face kidnapping/extortion risks from gangs controlling ~90% of Port-au-Prince.

sector_alerts.title: West Africa/Sahel lean season peak: FAO/WFP briefing Jul 9 — 54.8M at risk; Nigeria, Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Chad
sector_alerts.scope: countries/nigeria, countries/mali, countries/burkina-faso, countries/niger, countries/chad, countries/mauritania
sector_alerts.summary: July 6, 2026: FAO/WFP joint briefing scheduled July 9 on Sahel food crisis. Latest Cadre Harmonisé: up to 54.8M people across West Africa and Sahel at acute food insecurity risk June-August 2026 — up from 52.8M prior estimate. Nigeria: WFP warns imminent food assistance cuts; 35M+ food insecure. Mali: JNIM siege ongoing + new Jul 4 attacks. Burkina Faso: 4.5M in need. Nigeria VP and Obi both expressed concern over UN warnings (Jul 4).

sector_alerts.title: Ukraine: Jul 5–6 Kyiv strikes kill 21+ with no interceptors; civilian toll running 170/day — highest rate since 2023
sector_alerts.summary: July 5–6, 2026: Russia launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting Kyiv — 15 killed in city, 6 in region; Podilsky district hardest hit. None of 23 ballistic missiles intercepted (interceptor shortage). Separate strikes on Dnipro (17 killed) and Zaporizhzhia/Kherson. UN Human Rights Monitoring Mission: civilians killed or injured in Ukraine averaging 170/day in July 2026 — significantly higher than 2025, highest monthly rate since 2023.

entity_reviews.summary: RSF EL OBEID SIEGE — AL JAZEERA JUL 6: Al Jazeera published Jul 6: "El-Obeid under siege by RSF: Could this be Sudan's next el-Fasher?" — RSF systematically tightening siege. Amnesty International launched "City Under Siege, Children Under Fire" report Jul 1 documenting RSF crimes against humanity in North Darfur. UN Human Rights Council held landmark session Jul 3 to prevent El Obeid atrocities (HRW). HRW Jul 3: RSF drone strikes hit 16+ civilian targets including hospitals, schools, power stations; 45 civilians killed in 15 drone attacks Jun 6–28. Washington Post and UN News covered UN "red alert" (Jul 3). 500,000 civilians + 105,000 IDPs at siege risk.

entity_reviews.name: South Sudan
entity_reviews.summary: IPC PHASE 5 FAMINE RISK (ongoing Jul 6): 7.8M at IPC Phase 3+ (56% of population). 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate — in Akobo, Fangak, Nyirol, Uror (Jonglei) and Luakpiny/Nasir, Ulang (Upper Nile). Credible famine risk in four counties. 300,000 displaced in Jonglei; 200,000 from Akobo since March 6. 2.2M children acutely malnourished; 700,000 projected severe acute malnutrition through July. DRC Ebola now in Haut-Uele province (borders South Sudan). Cholera, malaria, measles compounding.

entity_reviews.name: Somalia
entity_reviews.summary: DEEPENING FOOD CRISIS + DROUGHT (Jul 6, sector sweep): 6.5M Somalis (~25% of population) facing high levels of acute food insecurity. 2M+ at IPC Phase 4 (emergency). 1.8M children under 5 expected to suffer acute malnutrition in 2026. Federal government declared national drought emergency Nov 2025. MSF teams documented urgent gaps in water and nutrition services in Afder and Shebelle zones. UNHCR, WFP, UNICEF, UNFPA all active with 2026 appeals.

entity_reviews.summary: PAKISTAN-AFGHANISTAN CONFLICT + CHRONIC CRISIS (Jul 6, sector sweep): ACAPS Jun 10 anticipatory note (in window) documented anticipated humanitarian impacts of Pakistan-Afghanistan conflict — 100,000+ residents in Bargi Matal/Kamdesh lacking humanitarian access; closure of roads due to cross-border fire. entity_reviews.summary: TPS WORK AUTHORIZATIONS EXPIRE JUL 10 — 350K HAITIANS AT RISK: DHS announced TPS work authorizations (Employment Authorization Documents) expire Jul 10, 2026.
